Lincoln Hwy upgrade from Cicero Ave. to Chicago Road, Cook County : environmental impact statement.

The proposal involves upgrading the Lincoln Highway (U.S. Route 30) from Cicero Avenue to Chicago Road in Cook County, Illinois. The current highway is outdated and congested, leading to high accident rates. The project aims to modernize the highway by expanding it to six lanes in some sections and four lanes in others, with a median for improved traffic flow. This upgrade is necessary to accommodate increasing traffic volumes and improve safety. A temporary easement will be used from the Woodrow Wilson Woods for construction, but no permanent land will be taken from the forest preserve.
Cook County, Illinois, from Cicero Avenue to Chicago Road

The Lincoln Highway upgrade project was proposed to address the outdated and congested conditions of U.S. Route 30 in Cook County, which had been linked to high accident rates. There was some public concern regarding the use of a temporary easement from the Woodrow Wilson Woods, but the project aimed to enhance safety and traffic flow. Ultimately, the project was seen as necessary to accommodate growing traffic demands.

"It is our opinion that the temporary use will not have any significant effect on the recreational opportunities of the community." — Letter from Cook County Forest Preserve District, September 4, 1974

"This project is already behind schedule. We were promised that land acquisition for the improvement would begin in 1973. The present surface of Highway 30 is deteriorating rapidly and cannot take any more delays." — Mayor George R. Bonick, City of Chicago Heights
